Script content:

#!/bin/bash
set -e

ARCH_FILE="/proc/sys/kernel/arch"
RULE_FILE="/etc/audit/rules.d/rmdir.rules"
TEMP_RULE_FILE="/tmp/rmdir.rules.tmp"

# Exit if aarch64
arch=$(cat "$ARCH_FILE" | tr -d '\n')
if [[ "$arch" == *aarch64* ]]; then
    echo "Architecture $arch: Skipping rmdir audit rule (not applicable)."
    exit 0
fi

# Determine arch values
arch_vals=()
if [[ "$arch" == *x86_64* ]]; then
    arch_vals=("b32" "b64")
elif [[ "$arch" =~ i[36]86 ]]; then
    arch_vals=("b32")
else
    echo "Unsupported architecture $arch"
    exit 0
fi

# Ensure rules.d dir exists
mkdir -p "$(dirname "$RULE_FILE")"

# Read existing content
if [[ -f "$RULE_FILE" ]]; then
    cp "$RULE_FILE" "$TEMP_RULE_FILE"
else
    touch "$TEMP_RULE_FILE"
fi

# Comment out existing rmdir lines
sed -i -e '/-S rmdir/ s/^/# /' "$TEMP_RULE_FILE"

# Prepare new rule lines, avoid duplicates
for arch_val in "${arch_vals[@]}"; do
    rule="-a always,exit -F arch=$arch_val -S rmdir -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k delete"
    # If rule already present (exact string), skip
    if ! grep -Fxq "$rule" "$TEMP_RULE_FILE"; then
        echo "$rule" >> "$TEMP_RULE_FILE"
    fi
done

# Ensure newline at end
echo "" >> "$TEMP_RULE_FILE"

# Write back file with correct permissions
mv "$TEMP_RULE_FILE" "$RULE_FILE"
chmod 0600 "$RULE_FILE"
chown root:root "$RULE_FILE"

# Apply the rule with auditctl
for arch_val in "${arch_vals[@]}"; do
    auditctl -a always,exit -F arch=$arch_val -S rmdir -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k delete || true
done

# Reload auditd
systemctl restart auditd

#!/bin/bash
set -e

ARCH_FILE="/proc/sys/kernel/arch"
RULE_FILE="/etc/audit/rules.d/rmdir.rules"
TEMP_RULE_FILE="/tmp/rmdir.rules.tmp"

# Exit if aarch64
arch=$(cat "$ARCH_FILE" | tr -d '\n')
if [[ "$arch" == *aarch64* ]]; then
    echo "Architecture $arch: Skipping rmdir audit rule (not applicable)."
    exit 0
fi

# Determine arch values
arch_vals=()
if [[ "$arch" == *x86_64* ]]; then
    arch_vals=("b32" "b64")
elif [[ "$arch" =~ i[36]86 ]]; then
    arch_vals=("b32")
else
    echo "Unsupported architecture $arch"
    exit 0
fi

# Ensure rules.d dir exists
mkdir -p "$(dirname "$RULE_FILE")"

# Read existing content
if [[ -f "$RULE_FILE" ]]; then
    cp "$RULE_FILE" "$TEMP_RULE_FILE"
else
    touch "$TEMP_RULE_FILE"
fi

# Comment out existing rmdir lines
sed -i -e '/-S rmdir/ s/^/# /' "$TEMP_RULE_FILE"

# Prepare new rule lines, avoid duplicates
for arch_val in "${arch_vals[@]}"; do
    rule="-a always,exit -F arch=$arch_val -S rmdir -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k delete"
    # If rule already present (exact string), skip
    if ! grep -Fxq "$rule" "$TEMP_RULE_FILE"; then
        echo "$rule" >> "$TEMP_RULE_FILE"
    fi
done

# Ensure newline at end
echo "" >> "$TEMP_RULE_FILE"

# Write back file with correct permissions
mv "$TEMP_RULE_FILE" "$RULE_FILE"
chmod 0600 "$RULE_FILE"
chown root:root "$RULE_FILE"

# Apply the rule with auditctl
for arch_val in "${arch_vals[@]}"; do
    auditctl -a always,exit -F arch=$arch_val -S rmdir -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k delete || true
done

# Reload auditd
systemctl restart auditd
